---
title: "Project Report"
author: "Dominic Wooldridge, Shane Waldron, Ben Robinson, Daniel Kwong, Mason Brehmer"
date: today
execute:
    warning: false
    message: false
format:
  html:
    theme: darkly
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
---

# Analysis

# Setup + Load Data

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve, auc, classification_report
```

# Read and Clean Column Names

```{python}
df = pd.read_csv("df_prepped.csv")

df.columns = (
    df.columns.str.strip()
    .str.lower()
    .str.replace(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", regex=True)
    .str.strip("_")
)

# drop leftover index columns like unnamed_0
df = df.loc[:, ~df.columns.str.contains(r"^unnamed", case=False)]

# drop household identifier (not a predictive/business feature)
df = df.drop(columns=["hh_id"], errors="ignore")

# convert bool predictors to int so sklearn pipeline handles cleanly
bool_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=["bool"]).columns
df[bool_cols] = df[bool_cols].astype(int)

print(df.shape)
print(df.head(3))


```

# Create Binary Targets (High YogExp / High DinExp)

```{python}
# Use 75th percentile cutoff for "high spender" classes
yog_cut = df["yogexp"].quantile(0.75)
din_cut = df["dinexp"].quantile(0.75)

df["high_yogexp"] = (df["yogexp"] >= yog_cut).astype(int)
df["high_dinexp"] = (df["dinexp"] >= din_cut).astype(int)

print("YogExp cutoff:", yog_cut)
print("DinExp cutoff:", din_cut)
print(df[["high_yogexp", "high_dinexp"]].mean())  # class balance
```

# Shared Helper Function (Train RF + ROC + Feature Importance)

```{python}
def run_rf_classifier(data, target_col, drop_cols):
    work = data[data[target_col].notna()].copy()

    X = work.drop(columns=drop_cols, errors="ignore")
    y = work[target_col].astype(int)

    num_cols = X.select_dtypes(include=np.number).columns.tolist()
    cat_cols = [c for c in X.columns if c not in num_cols]

    preprocess = ColumnTransformer([
        ("num", Pipeline([("imp", SimpleImputer(strategy="median"))]), num_cols),
        ("cat", Pipeline([
            ("imp", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
            ("oh", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore"))
        ]), cat_cols)
    ])

    clf = Pipeline([
        ("prep", preprocess),
        ("rf", RandomForestClassifier(
            n_estimators=500,
            random_state=42,
            n_jobs=-1,
            class_weight="balanced"
        ))
    ])

    X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
        X, y, test_size=0.25, random_state=42, stratify=y
    )

    clf.fit(X_train, y_train)

    proba = clf.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]
    pred = clf.predict(X_test)

    fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_test, proba)
    roc_auc = auc(fpr, tpr)

    # feature importance
    feat_names = clf.named_steps["prep"].get_feature_names_out()
    importances = clf.named_steps["rf"].feature_importances_
    fi = (
        pd.DataFrame({"feature": feat_names, "importance": importances})
        .sort_values("importance", ascending=False)
        .reset_index(drop=True)
    )

    print(classification_report(y_test, pred))
    print("AUC:", round(roc_auc, 4))

    return clf, fpr, tpr, roc_auc, fi
```


# Random Forest for YogExp (Classification)

```{python}
# Predict high_yogexp; remove leakage columns
drop_for_yog = ["yogexp", "dinexp", "high_yogexp", "high_dinexp"]

rf_yog, fpr_yog, tpr_yog, auc_yog, fi_yog = run_rf_classifier(
    df, target_col="high_yogexp", drop_cols=drop_for_yog
)
```

# ROC Curve for YogExp
```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 5))
plt.plot(fpr_yog, tpr_yog, label=f"RF YogExp (AUC={auc_yog:.3f})")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", label="Baseline")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve: High YogExp")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Feature Importance for YogExp

```{python}
top_n = 20
print(fi_yog.head(top_n))

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plot_df = fi_yog.head(top_n).iloc[::-1]
plt.barh(plot_df["feature"], plot_df["importance"])
plt.title("Top Feature Importance: High YogExp (RF)")
plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Random Forest for DinExp

```{python}
# Predict high_dinexp; remove leakage columns
drop_for_din = ["yogexp", "dinexp", "high_yogexp", "high_dinexp"]

rf_din, fpr_din, tpr_din, auc_din, fi_din = run_rf_classifier(
    df, target_col="high_dinexp", drop_cols=drop_for_din
)

```

# ROC Curve for DinExp


```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 5))
plt.plot(fpr_din, tpr_din, label=f"RF DinExp (AUC={auc_din:.3f})", color="tab:orange")
plt.plot([0, 1], [0, 1], "k--", label="Baseline")
plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve: High DinExp")
plt.legend(loc="lower right")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

# Feature Importance for DinExp
```{python}
top_n = 20
print(fi_din.head(top_n))

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plot_df = fi_din.head(top_n).iloc[::-1]
plt.barh(plot_df["feature"], plot_df["importance"], color="tab:orange")
plt.title("Top Feature Importance: High DinExp (RF)")
plt.xlabel("Importance")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
